summary: AI task exposure is high for actuarial analysis because recurring work includes modeling, data review, documentation, scenario testing, and report drafting. This does not mean the occupation will disappear.

facts.reader_safe_interpretation: High task exposure: AI can accelerate parts of modeling, analysis, checking, and documentation, while professional judgment and accountability remain human responsibilities.

items.label: Modeling and scenario analysis
items.interpretation: AI can assist with simulation setup, formula checking, documentation, and sensitivity analysis.

items.label: Professional accountability
items.interpretation: Risk assumptions, regulatory judgment, sign-off, and communication with stakeholders remain bounded by human expertise.

sources.boundary: Used as task evidence, not as an AI-risk score.

sources.source_name: Pew Research Center AI and O*NET methodology
sources.used_for: External example of mapping AI exposure to work activities
sources.boundary: Methodology reference only; does not publish this mock occupation score.

sources.source_name: GPTs are GPTs task exposure paper
sources.boundary: Calibration reference only; not an employment outcome prediction.

task_evidence.task_label: Analyze statistical data to estimate risk and uncertainty
task_evidence.captured_fact: Actuarial work includes recurring quantitative analysis, modeling, and risk-estimation tasks.
task_evidence.boundary: Mock example based on occupation task structure; production rows must capture exact source snippets.
task_evidence.exposure_relevance: Quantitative information-processing tasks can be accelerated by AI tools under human review.

task_evidence.task_label: Prepare reports and communicate risk findings
task_evidence.captured_fact: The occupation includes documentation and communication of analytical findings.
task_evidence.boundary: Mock example; production rows must capture exact source snippets.
task_evidence.exposure_relevance: Drafting, summarization, and consistency checking can be AI-assisted.

ai_exposure_score.rubric_source: FermatMind internal AI exposure rubric calibrated against task-level external AI exposure literature
ai_exposure_score.score_interpretation: A score of 8 indicates high task exposure to AI assistance or partial automation, not job-loss probability.

external_calibration_sources.source_name: Pew Research Center AI and O*NET methodology
external_calibration_sources.boundary: Methodology source, not an occupation-specific score source.

external_calibration_sources.source_name: GPTs are GPTs task exposure paper
external_calibration_sources.boundary: Research framing source, not a prediction that this occupation will disappear.

reader_boundary: This AI score is a task-exposure signal. It is not a prediction that actuaries will disappear, lose income, or lose employment.
limitations: Mock example only; not a production career asset.
Production rows must capture exact O*NET task snippets and pass trust audit before asset generation.
